CZ_NAME: UPPER GILA RIVER AND ARAVAIPA VALLEYS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Extreme Drought (D3) category ended in portions of the Upper Gila River and Aravaipa Valleys. The overall precipitation for Arizona ranking was 28th driest for the period of record for the month of January. Soil moisture readings continued to range between the 30th and 70th percentile ranking for the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: For the month of January, Graham county ranked the 24th driest for the period of record and Greenlee county ranked the 21st driest for the period of record. Soil moisture readings continued to range between the 30th and 70th percentile for the month of January.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system moved across Southeastern Arizona on January bringing light to moderate rainfall beginning at 5 AM on the 7th and continued off and on through 10 PM on the 8th. This heavy rainfall caused an anchor became loose leading to a power outage to 1400 people. A nearby CWOP station reported a rainfall total of 1.49 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall caused an anchor became loose leading to a power outage to 1400 people. A nearby CWOP station reported a rainfall total of 1.49 inches.

CZ_NAME: UPPER SANTA CRUZ RIVER AND ALTAR VALLEYS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong pressure gradient caused windy to high wind conditions across Southeastern Arizona on January 10th and 11th. A Wind Advisory was in effect from 5 PM on the 10th to 2 PM on January 11th in the mountains and from 5 AM to 2 PM Sunday for much of the valleys. The highest observed wind gust was 98 mph in the Santa Rita Mountains from the Mt. Hopkins RAWS on January 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mt. Hopkins RAWS recorded a measured peak gust of 98 mph. No damage was reported from the high winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold morning temperatures near normal across Southeastern Arizona. The Pima County Medical Examiner confirmed that cold and hypothermia was the primary factor in the cause of death for two individuals, one in Tucson and one in Sahuarita.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cold morning temperatures were 2 to 4 degrees below normal across Southeastern Arizona. Tucson International Airport ASOS reached a low temperature of 39 degrees Fahrenheit. The Pima County Medical Examiner confirmed that environmental cold exposure was the primary factor in the cause of death for two individuals, one in Tucson and one in Sahuarita.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A landspout tornado was spotted near Three Points, AZ. The tornado caused no damage or injury. The time and location of the tornado was estimated by radar. The landspout tornado was left unrated due to the remote location and would be difficult to survey.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A landspout tornado was spotted near Three Points, AZ. The tornado caused no damage or injury. The time and location of the tornado was estimated by radar. The landspout tornado was left unrated due to the remote location and would be difficult to survey.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system from off the Pacific Ocean brought low elevation rain and high elevation snow to northern Arizona above 5,000 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Public reported 9.5 inches of snow at Lukachukai.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system from off the Pacific Ocean brought low elevation rain and high elevation snow to northern Arizona above 5,000 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: National Weather Service employees measured a storm total snowfall amount of 8.0 inches at the NWS Flagstaff office in Bellemont.  ADOT employee reported 7.0 inches at their maintenance yard in Munds Park.  A qualified snow observer measured a storm total of 6.3 inches at the Flagstaff Airport (KFLG).
